subject: Physics
title: Galileo's Leaning Tower Drop
description: A short lesson to help you apply Galileo's Leaning Tower Drop.

hook.text: Legend says Galileo dropped two balls from the Leaning Tower of Pisa to prove heavy and light objects fall equally fast.

buildup.text: Aristotle taught that heavier objects fall faster. For 2,000 years, nobody seriously questioned this.

discovery.text: Galileo showed that in the absence of air resistance, all objects accelerate at the same rate regardless of mass.

twist.text: Historians debate whether the tower drop actually happened. But Galileo did prove the principle with ramp experiments.

climax.text: In 1971, Apollo 15 astronaut David Scott dropped a hammer and feather on the Moon. They hit the ground together.

punchline.text: Heavy or light, everything falls the same. Galileo proved Aristotle wrong.

quiz.question: What did the Apollo 15 hammer and feather drop demonstrate?
options: Without air resistance, all objects fall at the same rate
Heavier objects fall faster on the Moon
